stimulus:
The following text is adapted from Sara Teasdale’s 1920 poem “The Storm.”

The Wind comes striding down the hill
In boots of leather and a cloak of grey,
And shakes the trees with restless hand
As though impatient with the day.
He stoops beside the silver pool
To wash the dust of distance from his brow,
And lifts his voice to call the rain
Like one who summons servants now.
stem: Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?

answerOptions.content: It alternates descriptions of wind in a forest with descriptions of wind on a plain.
It sketches an image of an approaching storm, then an image of its aftermath.
It makes an extended comparison of wind to a human being.
It portrays how wind changes from one season of the year to the next.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer because it most accurately describes the overall structure of the text. Throughout the poem, the speaker characterizes the wind as if it were a person who wears clothing (“boots of leather and a cloak of grey”), uses its hands to shake trees, washes its brow at a pool, and calls servants. The text is thus structured as an extended comparison of wind to a human being.

Choice A is incorrect because the text does not name particular locations like forest and plain; instead, it presents a single image of wind as a person. Choice B is incorrect because the text does not refer to a storm’s aftermath. Choice D is incorrect because the text does not describe seasonal changes; it presents one continuous image.
